summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, AI / computing, Education / pedagogy. The strongest items in this run are Cuba has run out of diesel and fuel oil, energy minister says, as US blockade pushes island to brink, New York man found guilty of acting as an unregistered agent of China, and FCC angers small carriers by helping AT&T and Starlink buy EchoStar spectrum.

mainItems.title: Cuba has run out of diesel and fuel oil, energy minister says, as US blockade pushes island to brink
mainItems.summary: Minister Vicente de la O Levy says ‘Cuba is open to anyone that wants to sell us fuel’ as rolling power blackouts increase Cuba has completely run out of diesel and fuel oil, the country’s energy minister said on Wednesday, as Havana faces…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: New York man found guilty of acting as an unregistered agent of China
mainItems.summary: Lu Jianwang was accused of operating a ‘secret police station’ in Manhattan’s Chinatown at the behest of Beijing A New York man was found guilty on Wednesday of acting as an unregistered agent of the Chinese government after he was accused…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: FCC angers small carriers by helping AT&T and Starlink buy EchoStar spectrum
mainItems.summary: Approval is no surprise after FCC chair pressured EchoStar to sell licenses.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: AMD’s best CPU tech for gamers is coming to workstations too
mainItems.summary: For the first time, AMD is including its 3D V-Cache tech in its commercial workstation processors with a refreshed line of Ryzen PRO 9000 series chips.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Kids' test scores began declining way before COVID. These schools are making gains
mainItems.summary: The annual Education Scorecard shows the nation's schools still rebounding from serious losses in math and reading, but it also found those declines began well before the pandemic.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: AI invades Princeton, where 30% of students cheat—but peers won't snitch
mainItems.summary: Old "honor code" systems are under strain.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Beware what you tell your AI chatbot. It’s not a shrink – it’s a snitch | Arwa Mahdawi
mainItems.summary: In a case of ‘oh dear diary’, the OpenAI president Greg Brockman is having to read extracts from his musings about Elon Musk in court.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Alabaster Dawn is the kind of 2.5D action-RPG spectacle that makes you question the need for "true" 3D
mainItems.summary: One of the grievous burdens the Heroic Videogames Journalist must shoulder is seeing a couple dozen Cool New Games each week, and only having the time to write about two or three.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Mark Zuckerberg announces ‘completely private’ encrypted Meta AI chat
mainItems.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg says its new Incognito Chat is "the first major AI product where there is no log of your conversations stored on servers.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: The physics of how Olympic weightlifters exploit barbell's "whip"
mainItems.summary: The type of bar matters when it comes to how it bends and recoils, but why is still a mystery.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to systems, infrastructure, and technical risk.

mainItems.title: Adaptation of Enid Blyton’s The Magic Faraway Tree Gets a Release Date in New Trailer
mainItems.summary: Vertical announced the release day along with a new trailer to celebrate the news The post Adaptation of Enid Blyton’s The Magic Faraway Tree Gets a Release Date in New Trailer appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: Podcast: The Chinese Deepfake Software Powering Scams
mainItems.summary: We got Haotian AI, the Chinese-language deepfake software powering scams. We also talk about a man finding $1 million of Yu-Gi-Oh cards, and how the AI hard drive shortage is impacting internet archiving.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: French hantavirus patient is critically ill as outbreak grows to 11
mainItems.summary: A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

new.title: Constellation
new.why: A little more cerebral and mood-heavy, but it scratches the identity / reality-slippage itch.

new.title: The Devil’s Hour
new.why: Dark, twisty, and structurally ambitious without feeling like homework.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

older.title: Continuum
older.why: A cleaner old-school sci-fi fit if you want procedural momentum wrapped around time-travel politics.

older.title: Person of Interest
older.why: Starts procedural and then quietly becomes one of the smartest AI / surveillance shows on television.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: 12 unexpected sourdough bread recipes that aren't crusty loaves: From sourdough flatbread to sourdough milk bread — and even sourdough babka.
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing, the kind of bread-nerd rabbit hole worth keeping.
